# SOURCE: Butt In Seats (BIS)

> **Person:** Dan Kennedy + Bill Glazer
> **Program:** Butt In Seats and Bucks in the Bank
> **Year:** 2011
> **Publisher:** Glazer-Kennedy Insider's Circle
> **Type:** Live Presentation + Transcripts (Info Summit bonus session)
> **Files:** 6 (2 CDs, 2 DVDs, 1 Exhibits Manual, 1 Full Transcript)
> **Total Size:** ~388K bytes
> **DNA Elements Extracted:** 90
> **Version:** 1.0.0
> **Date:** 2026-03-16

---

## Overview

"Butt In Seats and Bucks in the Bank" is a comprehensive program on seminar/event marketing delivered by Dan Kennedy (psychology of attendance) and Bill Glazer (tactical execution, logistics, choreography). Presented at a Glazer-Kennedy Info Summit with nearly 50 years of collective experience and over $100M generated from seminars attended by over 1 million people.

## Content Structure

| File | Speaker | Topic | Size |
|------|---------|-------|------|
| CD 1 | Bill Glazer | 7 Chief Factors + 39 Marketing Boosters (Part 1) | 39K |
| CD 2 | Bill Glazer | 39 Boosters (Part 2) + Logistics + Choreography | 40K |
| DVD 1 | Dan Kennedy | Psychology of Seminar Attendance | 71K |
| DVD 2 | Bill Glazer | Extended logistics + Frank Kern + Pre-registration | 84K |
| Exhibits Manual | Both | Slide deck/visual exhibits (147 pages, mostly images) | 9K |
| Full Transcript | Both | Official written transcript (60 pages) | 145K |

## Top Frameworks

1. **7 Chief Factors of Seminar Attendance** (FW-DK-832) — Relationship, Influence, Relevant Topic, List Knowledge, List Size, Marketing Funnel, Mindset
2. **Seminar Marketing BOOSTERS System** (FW-DK-833) — 7 categories, 39 Swipe & Deploy examples
3. **Repurpose with Power System** (FW-DK-834) — One main sales letter → 18+ derivative media pieces
4. **Seminar Choreography Maximization** (FW-DK-842) — 10 choreography elements for profit maximization
5. **Unique Experience Elements Checklist** (FW-DK-843) — 12 elements that transform seminar into experience

## Top Heuristics (Numeric)

| Heuristic | Value | Source |
|-----------|-------|--------|
| Marketing touches per major event | 90+ | HEUR-DK-920 |
| Lead time for event marketing | 6 months | HEUR-DK-921 |
| Celebrity attendance multiplier (Gene Simmons) | +200 people | HEUR-DK-922 |
| Pre-registrations at event (best case) | 647/1200 | HEUR-DK-923 |
| Hotel coffee per gallon | $60 | HEUR-DK-924 |
| Hotel contract negotiation cycle | 6 weeks | HEUR-DK-925 |
| Emails from one sales letter | 18 over 6 weeks | HEUR-DK-926 |
| Cover letter segmentation versions | 8 versions | HEUR-DK-937 |
| Setup days before event | 4 (Monday→Thursday) | HEUR-DK-928 |

## Key Philosophies

- "Seminars must become UNIQUE EXPERIENCES — the seminar-as-information-delivery model is dead"
- "The person who cannot afford it WILL come — never assume the broke prospect won't buy"
- "The hotel is your enemy from the moment you start talking — negotiate everything"
- "You must EARN THE RIGHT to ask people to do business with you"
- "Celebrity makes you STOP AND LOOK — the infomercial principle applied to events"
- "The job of writing copy is easier than you think — create ONE main letter, then repurpose"
